title: Local review flags redundant provider lifecycle logging additions

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark whether local review now catches redundant lifecycle/provider logging additions while preserving the accepted remove-from-state exception.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic review case focused on the new lifecycle logging policy and the accepted remove-from-state exception.

description: A synthetic local-review scenario where an `internal/**/*.go` change adds generic provider-side lifecycle logging such as `Import check`, `Creating`, `Reading`, `Updating`, or `Deleting`. The correct review should flag those additions when they only duplicate Terraform core/provider-native logging, while not objecting to a narrow remove-from-state diagnostic.

scope.notes: Synthetic review case focused on the new lifecycle logging policy and the accepted remove-from-state exception.

mustCatch.description: The review should treat newly added generic `Import check`, `Creating`, `Reading`, `Updating`, or `Deleting` logging as a reviewable issue when it adds no unique diagnostic value beyond Terraform core/provider-native logging.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not flag the narrow remove-from-state/not-found diagnostic pattern when it is the distinct debugging-value exception allowed by the implementation guidance.

notes: Use this case to benchmark whether local review now catches redundant lifecycle/provider logging additions while preserving the accepted remove-from-state exception.
